site.title: Macro Daily
site.subtitle: Daily macro briefing powered by the local research framework

latest.headline: Growth repair is still partial, while liquidity and risk appetite remain the main bridge into pricing.
latest.summary: Today's sample brief shows how the macro framework can turn policy, data, and global headlines into one transmission-based daily note.

sections.title: Today's macro line
items: The current working line is not a full-cycle recovery yet; it is a partial repair story that still depends on liquidity and confidence transmission.
The framework starts from the core contradiction, then checks whether policy, rates, credit, FX, and risk appetite are actually moving in the same direction.

sections.title: Economic data focus
items: Watch for the next domestic PMI, credit pulse, and property activity update to confirm whether repair is broadening or staying narrow.
External data should be read mainly through rate and dollar implications rather than as isolated headlines.

sections.title: Policy and official signals
items: Policy language matters only if it creates a visible bridge into rates, credit, or demand-sensitive sectors.
The daily note should rank policy signals by transmission power instead of repeating every announcement equally.

sections.title: International and geopolitical news
items: Global and geopolitical developments enter the note through risk premium, trust, and cross-market capital flow channels.
Not every geopolitical event deserves headline weight; only events that reprice risk or redirect flows belong in the main line.

sections.title: Transmission map
items: Base path: policy -> rates -> credit -> FX/liquidity -> risk appetite -> asset pricing.
Fast paths can also start from funds, sentiment, or geopolitics, but they still need to reconnect to the same macro map.

sections.title: Risk and falsification
items: If rates or credit do not confirm, today's constructive line should be downgraded.
If risk assets move only on positioning while mid-chain variables stay weak, treat the move as trading heat instead of durable confirmation.

sections.title: Next watchlist
items: Domestic data: PMI, credit, property sales.
Policy layer: central bank and major ministry statements.
External layer: Fed path, dollar direction, major geopolitical escalation signals.
